serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 4 pieces of chicken drumlets (approximately 200 g), as sold by the restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, drumette, meat and skin, cooked, roasted (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient profile derived from USDA FoodData Central roasted chicken drumette (meat and skin). Carbohydrate, sugar, and sodium values adjusted upward to reflect honey and BBQ glaze contribution, consistent with the input record values (energy 240 kcal, protein 24 g, fat 14 g, carbohydrate 5 g, sugar 3 g, sodium 600 mg per 100 g). Input record provenance is ai_generated with confidence 0.7; values are plausible and consistent with USDA roasted chicken drumette baseline plus glaze addition. Values retained as best available estimate.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for honey BBQ glazed chicken drumlets. GI estimated at approximately 40 (low) based on analogous high-protein, low-carbohydrate cooked chicken dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dominant macronutrient is protein (24 g/100 g) with only 5 g/100 g carbohydrate from honey and BBQ glaze; protein and fat substantially blunt glycaemic response. GL per 200 g serving = (40 × 10 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 4.0; per 100 g = 2.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 600 mg per 100 g; a 200 g serving delivers 1200 mg sodium, representing approximately 52–60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this dish to no more than one serving per meal and request reduced-sodium BBQ sauce and soy sauce to bring sodium below 400 mg per 100 g; avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate is only 5 g per 100 g (10 g per 200 g serving) with a low estimated GI of approximately 40, yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 4 per serving; high protein content (24 g/100 g) further blunts postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for most individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in standard portion sizes; monitor for variable sugar content across different BBQ sauce preparations and avoid additional sweet dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4 g per 100 g and sodium at 600 mg per 100 g are the primary cardiovascular concerns; trans fat is present at 0.1 g per 100 g (low but non-zero); cholesterol is 100 mg per 100 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 2 drumlets (approximately 100 g) per meal, choose preparations with reduced sodium and no added trans fats, and balance with high-fibre vegetables to offset the saturated fat and sodium load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium at 600 mg per 100 g exceeds the 400 mg per 100 g threshold associated with worsening RA-related tissue swelling; nightshade-derived spices (paprika, chilli) may aggravate solanine-sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request reduced soy sauce and low-sodium BBQ sauce to bring sodium below 400 mg per 100 g, and monitor personal response to nightshade-derived spices in the marinade.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Oven-Baked Honey BBQ Chicken Drumlets is a popular Western-American style dish adapted for the Singapore food service market, consisting of four pieces of chicken drumlets (the upper portion of the chicken wing, also called drumettes) that are marinated and oven-baked rather than deep-fried, then coated in a sweet and smoky honey BBQ glaze made from honey, BBQ sauce, soy sauce, garlic, paprika, and brown sugar. The oven-baking method produces a tender, juicy interior with a caramelised, sticky glaze exterior, delivering a balance of sweet, smoky, and savoury flavours. Each 200 g serving (4 pieces) provides approximately 480 kcal, 48 g protein, 28 g fat, and only 10 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate dish. Sodium is elevated at 600 mg per 100 g (1200 mg per serving), which is a significant concern for individuals managing hypertension or rheumatoid arthritis. The dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate content and high protein, which blunts glycaemic response. Cardiovascular disease patients should moderate portion size due to saturated fat (4 g/100 g) and sodium content. The dish is halal-friendly and dairy-free. It is commonly served as a starter, snack, or side dish in casual dining and food delivery contexts in Singapore.
common_user_queries: Is honey BBQ chicken drumlets healthy?
How many calories in 4 pieces of chicken drumlets?
Can diabetics eat honey BBQ drumlets?
Is oven-baked chicken drumlets high in sodium?
What is the protein content of chicken drumlets?
Are honey BBQ drumlets suitable for hypertension?
How much fat is in BBQ chicken drumlets?

Values are internally consistent and plausible but represent an adapted estimate rather than a direct source match.
